docs.title: Docs Stack – Material
docs.text:
Status: Live (v1.0)

A professional documentation system built with MkDocs and Material for MkDocs.

docs.title: What this project provides
docs.text:
• Production-grade documentation structure
  • Clean navigation and search
  • Version-controlled content

docs.title: Who should use this
docs.text:
• Backend developers
  • API engineers
  • Open-source contributors

docs.title: Featured Projects
docs.text:
Explore the documentation for my key projects:

  • Portfolio React: Modern developer portfolio with admin dashboard.
  • Noirel Ecommerce: Premium e-commerce application.
  • Time Lens Python: Mindset tool to reframe time perception.
  • Salary Reality Checker: AI-powered salary analytics platform.
  • Todo Redux Advanced: Advanced Todo app with Redux Toolkit.
  • Fullstack Blog JWT: Secure full-stack blog with Docker.
  • Student Management: Comprehensive Django-based student system.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: The projects in this portfolio primarily adhere to modern web development architectures, ensuring scalability, maintainability, and performance.

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
• Component-Based: Built mainly with React.js, emphasizing reusable UI components.
  • State Management: Utilizing Context API, Redux Toolkit, or Zustand depending on complexity.
  • Styling: Tailwind CSS is the standard for responsive and utility-first styling.

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
• REST API: Most backends (Django, FastAPI, Node.js) expose RESTful endpoints.
  • MVC / MVT: Django projects follow the Model-View-Template pattern (similar to MVC).
  • Microservices vs Monolith: Smaller tools (Time Lens) are microservices/functions, while larger platforms (Student Management, Noirel) are modular monoliths.

docs.title: Database
docs.text:
• Relational: PostgreSQL is the preferred choice for complex data relations (Salary Checker, Student Management).
  • NoSQL: MongoDB or JSON Server is used for lighter or rapid-prototyping projects (Portfolio).

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
• Frontend: Vercel / Netlify.
  • Backend: Render / Railway / Heroku.
  • Containerization: Docker is used to standardize runtime environments.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Most projects in this portfolio use environment variables for configuration to ensure security and flexibility.

docs.title: Common Environment Variables
docs.text: While each project has unique needs, you will commonly encounter these:

docs.title: Database
docs.text:
• DATABASE_URL: Connection string for PostgreSQL or MongoDB.
  • DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD: Individual connection parameters.

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• SECRET_KEY: Used for cryptographic signing (Django/Flask).
  • JWT_SECRET: Secret key for signing JSON Web Tokens.
  • ADMIN_PASSWORD: Default password for initial admin accounts.

docs.title: External Services
docs.text:
• VITE_API_URL: Backend API URL for frontend applications.
  • STRIPE_SECRET_KEY / RAZORPAY_KEY_ID: Payment gateway credentials.
  • EMAIL_HOST_USER, EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD: SMTP credentials for sending emails.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Copy the example env file (often named .env.example).
  2. Rename it to .env.
  3. Fill in your specific values.

[!WARNING] Never commit your .env file to version control. It is excluded by default in .gitignore.

docs.title: Conventions
docs.text: To maintain consistency across projects, the following conventions are generally observed.

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Formatting: Prettier for JavaScript/React; Black for Python.
  • Linting: ESLint for JS; Flake8/Pylint for Python.

docs.title: Commit Messages
docs.text:
We follow the Conventional Commits specification:

  • feat: ... for new features.
  • fix: ... for bug fixes.
  • docs: ... for documentation updates.
  • style: ... for formatting changes.
  • refactor: ... for code refactoring.

docs.title: Branching
docs.text:
• main / master: Production-ready code.
  • dev: Development branch (if applicable).
  • feature/<name>: New feature development.
  • bugfix/<name>: Bug fixes.

docs.title: File Naming
docs.text:
• React Components: PascalCase (e.g., Button.tsx, Navbar.jsx).
  • JS/TS Utilities: camelCase (e.g., formatDate.ts).
  • Python Modules: snake_case (e.g., utils.py, models.py).

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Welcome to the documentation for Ashif's Project Portfolio. This collection showcases a diverse range of open-source projects, from full-stack web applications to utility tools and data analysis platforms.

docs.title: What's Included?
docs.text:
This site mainly documents the following key projects:

  • Portfolio React: A personal developer portfolio with an admin dashboard.
  • Noirel Ecommerce: A high-end e-commerce experience.
  • Salary Checker: AI-driven salary insights and predictions.
  • Time Lens: A psychological tool for time perception.

Each project documentation includes its specific features, tech stack, and installation guides.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Most projects in this portfolio rely on the following technologies. Ensure you have them installed before running them locally:

  • Node.js (v18+ recommended) for React/Next.js projects.
  • Python (v3.10+) for Django/FastAPI backends.
  • Docker for containerized applications.
  • Git for version control.

docs.title: Installation Overview
docs.text: Since this documentation covers multiple independent projects, installation steps vary by repository. However, most follow a standard pattern.

docs.title: General Installation Steps
docs.text:
1. Clone the Repository: bash git clone https://github.com/ashif-ek/<repo-name>.git cd <repo-name>

  2. Install Dependencies:

    • For JavaScript/Video projects: bash npm install # or yarn install
    • For Python projects: bash python -m venv venv source venv/bin/activate # Windows: venv\Scripts\activate pip install -r requirements.txt

  3. Environment Configuration:

    • Most projects require a .env file. Check the specific project documentation or README.md for required variables.

  4. Run the Application:

    • JS: npm run dev
    • Python (Django): python manage.py runserver
    • Python (FastAPI): uvicorn main:app --reload

docs.title: Project-Specific Guides
docs.text:
For detailed instructions, refer to the specific project pages:

  • Portfolio React Installation
  • Noirel Ecommerce Installation
  • Salary Checker Setup
  • Time Lens Setup

docs.title: Authentication API
docs.text: Secure access handling across applications.

docs.title: JWT Implementation
docs.text: Projects like Fullstack Blog and Student Management use JSON Web Tokens (JWT).

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
1. Login: POST /api/token/
    • Payload: {username, password}
    • Response: {access, refresh}
  2. Access Protected Routes:
    • Header: Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
  3. Refresh Token: POST /api/token/refresh/
    • Payload: {refresh}

docs.title: Admin Access
docs.text: Admin panels are protected via session auth or specific internal roles. * Django Admin: /admin/ (Standard Django auth). * Portfolio Admin: Custom implementation.

docs.title: Database Schema & API
docs.text: How data is structured and accessed.

docs.title: User Model (Common)
docs.text:
• id: UUID or Integer.
  • username: String, unique.
  • email: String, unique.
  • is_active: Boolean.

docs.title: Domain Specific
docs.text:
• Ecommerce: Product, Order, CartItem.
  • Education: Student, Course, Enrollment.
  • Salary: SalaryData (role, experience, city, amount).

docs.title: ORM Interaction
docs.text:
• Django ORM: Model.objects.filter(...)
  • SQLAlchemy: session.query(Model).filter(...)
  • Mongoose/JSON: Direct object manipulation.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: Standardized error responses.

docs.title: HTTP Status Codes
docs.text:
• 200 OK: Success.
  • 201 Created: Resource successfully created.
  • 400 Bad Request: Validation error.
  • 401 Unauthorized: Missing or invalid token.
  • 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions.
  • 404 Not Found: Resource does not exist.
  • 500 Server Error: Internal application failure.

docs.title: Error Response Format
docs.text:
{
  "status": "error",
  "code": 400,
  "message": "Invalid password format",
  "details": {
    "field": "password",
    "issue": "Must contain at least one number"
  }
}

docs.title: API Documentation
docs.text: This section provides an overview of the APIs exposed by the various backend services.

docs.title: Implementation Details
docs.text: Most backends in this portfolio are self-documenting: * FastAPI & Django (DRF): Automatically generate Swagger/OpenAPI documentation. * Visit /docs or /redoc on the running backend server to explore endpoints interactively.

docs.title: Salary Checker
docs.text:
• GET /salary/insights: Retrieve percentile data.
  • POST /salary/predict: ML-based salary prediction.

docs.title: Portfolio Backend
docs.text:
• GET /projects: List all projects.
  • POST /messages: Submit contact form data.

docs.title: Noirel Ecommerce
docs.text:
• GET /products: Fetch catalog.
  • POST /cart: Manage shopping cart.
  • POST /orders: Place a new order.

Refer to the individual project repositories for the most up-to-date API contracts.

docs.title: Customization
docs.text: How to modify and extend the projects.

docs.title: Tailwind CSS Theme
docs.text: Most styled projects use tailwind.config.js. You can customize: * Colors: Modify theme.extend.colors to change the palette. * Fonts: Update fontFamily settings.

docs.title: React Components
docs.text: UI components are typically found in src/components. * Reusability: Components are built to be generic. * Props: Check the interface definitions (TypeScript) or PropTypes (JS) to understand available options.

docs.title: Backend Logic
docs.text:
• Django: Apps are modular. To add specific business logic, create a new app: python manage.py startapp <name>.
  • FastAPI: Add new routers in the api/ directory and include them in main.py.

docs.title: Deployment Strategies
docs.text: This guide outlines how to deploy the various applications in this portfolio.

docs.title: Frontend Deployment (Vercel)
docs.text:
Most React/Next.js frontends are optimized for Vercel.

  1. Connect GitHub: detailed instructions...
  2. Import Project: Select the repository.
  3. Build Settings:
    • Framework Preset: Vite or Next.js
    • Build Command: npm run build
    • Output Directory: dist or .next
  4. Environment Variables: copy from your local .env.

docs.title: Backend Deployment (Render)
docs.text:
Render is the preferred choice for Python and Node.js backends.

  1. Create Web Service.
  2. Connect Repo.
  3. Runtime: Python 3 or Node.
  4. Build Command:
    • Python: pip install -r requirements.txt
    • Node: npm install
  5. Start Command:
    • FastAPI: uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT
    • Django: gunicorn core.wsgi:application

docs.title: Docker Deployment
docs.text:
For containerized apps (like fullstack-blog-jwt-docker), use the Dockerfile:

docker build -t my-app .
docker run -p 8000:8000 my-app

docs.title: Local Development Guide
docs.text: This guide covers the general workflow for working with the projects locally.

docs.title: Prerequisite Tools
docs.text: Ensure you have the following installed: * Git * Node.js (LTS version) * Python (3.10+) * VS Code (Recommended IDE)

docs.title: Setting Up a Project
docs.text:
1. Clone: git clone <url>
  2. Env Vars: Create .env from .env.example.
  3. Dependencies: npm install or pip install -r requirements.txt.

docs.title: Running Servers
docs.text:
• Frontend: Usually runs on http://localhost:5173 (Vite) or 3000 (Next.js).
  • Backend: Usually runs on http://localhost:8000 (Django/FastAPI) or 5000 (Node/Express).

docs.title: Debugging
docs.text:
• Frontend: Use React Developer Tools.
  • Backend: Check terminal logs for stack traces. Use pdb for Python debugging if needed.

docs.title: 1. Project Overview
docs.text:
The Fullstack Blog is a production-hardened web application designed to demonstrate secure, scalable, and containerized architecture. It bridges a high-performance Django REST API backend with a dynamic React frontend, orchestrated entirely via Docker.

This project goes beyond simple CRUD, focusing on enterprise-grade authentication, security best practices, and DevOps automation. It is engineered to handle high concurrency (load tested at 1000+ RPS) and maintain strict security standards.

docs.title: Architectural Pattern
docs.text:
Containerized Microservices Approach

The application is composed of isolated services running in Docker containers, communicating via an internal bridge network.

Client (Vite/React) ↔ Nginx (Reverse Proxy) ↔ Django application ↔ PostgreSQL ↕ Redis (Token Blacklist)

docs.title: Design Philosophy
docs.text:
• Security First: Zero-trust architecture with short-lived access tokens.
  • Statelessness: RESTful API design ensuring horizontal scalability.
  • Infrastructure as Code: Entire environment defined in docker-compose.yml.

docs.title: Backend Infrastructure
docs.text:
• Framework: Django 4.2 + Django REST Framework (DRF)
  • Authentication: Simple JWT (Custom Implementation)
  • Database: PostgreSQL 15
  • Caching/Queue: Redis (Alpine)
  • Containerization: Docker & Docker Compose
  • Server: Gunicorn (WSGI)

docs.title: Frontend Experience
docs.text:
• Framework: React 18
  • Build System: Vite 4 (HMR optimized)
  • Styling: Tailwind CSS 3
  • State: Context API + Custom Hooks
  • HTTP Client: Axios (w/ Interceptors)

docs.title: 4. Security & Authentication Module
docs.text: The core strength of this project is its robust authentication system, replicating industry standards.

docs.title: 4.1 Dual-Token Strategy
docs.text:
We implement a secure rotation mechanism to balance UX and security:

  • Access Token: Short-lived (15 minutes). Used for API authorization.
  • Refresh Token: Long-lived (7 days). Used securely to obtain new access tokens.

docs.title: 4.2 Security Features
docs.text:
• Token Blacklisting: Logout invalidate tokens immediately by storing them in Redis with TTL.
  • Password Hashing: Bcrypt implementation with salt for credential storage.
  • Clock Skew Tolerance: Configured (±30s) to handle distributed server time drifts.
  • Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): Decorator-based permission checks for Admin vs. Standard User.

docs.title: 5.1 Content Management
docs.text:
• CRUD Operations: Full lifecycle mangement for blog posts.
  • Rich Text Support: Markdown/HTML content handling.
  • Owner Permissions: Custom permission classes ensure users can only edit/delete their own posts.

docs.title: 5.2 DevOps & Orchestration
docs.text:
The docker-compose setup orchestrates the lifecycle of all services:

  1. db: PostgreSQL container with persistent volume.
  2. redis: In-memory store for session/token management.
  3. backend: Python service. Waits for db to be healthy (healthcheck implementation).
  4. frontend: Node.js service. Proxies API requests to backend in dev mode.

docs.title: 6. Performance Engineering
docs.text:
• Load Testing: Validated with Apache Bench to sustain 500+ requests/second on standard hardware.
  • Database Optimization: Indexed fields for author lookups and timestamp sorting.
  • Frontend Optimization:
    • Vite for O(1) build times.
    • Tailwind utility classes for minimal CSS bundle size.

docs.title: 7. API Specification
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description Auth POST /api/auth/token/ Obtain Access & Refresh Pair ❌ POST /api/auth/token/refresh/ Rotate Access Token ❌ POST /api/auth/logout/ Blacklist Token (Redis) ✅ GET /api/posts/ List all Pubilc Posts ❌ POST /api/posts/ Create Post ✅ PATCH /api/posts/<id>/ Update Post (Owner Only) ✅

docs.title: Docker (Recommended)
docs.text:
One command deployment for the entire stack:

git clone https://github.com/ashif-ek/fullstack-blog-jwt-docker.git
cd fullstack-blog-jwt-docker
docker-compose up --build -d
  • Frontend: http://localhost:5173
  • API: http://localhost:8000/api

docs.title: Manual Setup (Dev)
docs.text:
1. Backend: bash pip install -r requirements.txt python manage.py migrate python manage.py runserver
  2. Frontend: bash npm install npm run dev

docs.title: 9. Engineering Checklist
docs.text:
This project demonstrates maturity in:

  • ✅ Containerization: Full Docker support.
  • ✅ Security: OWASP-aligned auth flows.
  • ✅ Scalability: Stateless backend design.
  • ✅ Code Quality: Modular directory structure and separations of concerns.

docs.title: 1. Project Overview
docs.text:
Noirel is a production-grade, headless e-commerce platform engineered to deliver a high-performance luxury shopping experience. The system separates frontend and backend concerns using a modern React-based client and a secure Django REST API backend.

The architecture emphasizes:

  • Scalability
  • Performance optimization
  • Secure authentication
  • Cloud-native deployment
  • Clean system design
  • Real-world payment processing

It replicates modern commerce infrastructure used in industry-level platforms.

docs.title: Architectural Pattern
docs.text:
Headless Architecture (Decoupled Client–Server)

Frontend and backend communicate strictly via REST APIs.

Client (React) → Nginx → Gunicorn → Django → PostgreSQL ↓ AWS S3

docs.title: Why Headless?
docs.text:
• Independent scaling of frontend and backend
  • Deployment flexibility
  • Clean API contract
  • Frontend portability (future mobile app possible)

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
• Framework: React 19
  • Build Tool: Vite 6
  • Styling: Tailwind CSS 4
  • Routing: React Router 7
  • State Management: Context API (Auth, Cart, Wishlist, Search)
  • HTTP Client: Axios (JWT interceptors)
  • Animation: Framer Motion
  • Charts: Recharts
  • Icons: Lucide React
  • PWA Support: vite-plugin-pwa

Key Frontend Design Decisions:

  • Component isolation to reduce unnecessary re-renders
  • Route-based lazy loading
  • Memoized context values
  • Chunk splitting for caching efficiency
  • WebP image optimization
  • Mobile-first responsive design

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
• Framework: Django 5
  • API: Django REST Framework
  • Authentication: Simple JWT
  • Database: PostgreSQL
  • Image Processing: Pillow
  • Payment Gateway: Razorpay
  • API Docs: drf-spectacular
  • Server: Gunicorn

docs.title: Infrastructure
docs.text:
• Frontend Hosting: Vercel (Global CDN)
  • Backend Hosting: AWS EC2 (Ubuntu)
  • Reverse Proxy: Nginx
  • Media Storage: AWS S3
  • Database Hosting: AWS RDS PostgreSQL
  • CI/CD: GitHub Actions

docs.title: 4.1 Authentication Module
docs.text:
• JWT-based authentication
  • Access + refresh token flow
  • Axios interceptor for auto token injection
  • Protected routes in frontend
  • Role-based access (Admin / User)

Security Features:

  • Strict CORS
  • Throttling on sensitive endpoints
  • Hashed passwords
  • Token validation middleware

docs.title: 4.2 Product Module
docs.text:
Features:

  • Product CRUD (Admin)
  • Category-based filtering
  • Search functionality
  • Best Sellers / New Arrivals logic
  • Stock tracking
  • Image upload to S3

Database Optimization:

  • Indexed fields (is_active, created_at)
  • Foreign key indexing
  • Efficient query selection

docs.title: 4.3 Cart System
docs.text:
Server-side persistent cart.

Flow:

  1. User adds item
  2. API validates stock
  3. Item stored in cart table
  4. Response updates UI

Features:

  • Quantity updates
  • Real-time sync
  • Prevent overstock ordering

docs.title: 4.4 Order & Checkout Module
docs.text:
Multi-step Checkout:

  • Address selection
  • Order summary
  • Payment initiation

Order Creation:

  • Atomic transaction
  • Stock validation
  • Total calculation
  • Payment signature verification
with transaction.atomic():
    validate_stock()
    create_order()
    reduce_inventory()

Prevents race conditions and inconsistent state.

docs.title: 4.5 Payment Integration
docs.text:
• Gateway: Razorpay

Flow:

  1. Order created server-side
  2. Payment order generated
  3. Client completes payment
  4. Signature verified server-side
  5. Order marked as paid

Security:

  • Server-side signature validation
  • Payment amount verification

docs.title: 4.6 Admin Dashboard
docs.text:
• Revenue analytics (Recharts)
  • Order status distribution
  • Product management
  • Stock management
  • User control (block/unblock)

Optimized for operational control.

docs.title: Frontend Optimizations
docs.text:
1. Image Optimization
    • Converted assets to WebP
    • Reduced payload size significantly
  2. Code Splitting
    • Vite manual chunk configuration: vendor, ui, charts
    • Improves caching and load performance.
  3. Memoization Strategy
    • useMemo for context value
    • Isolated modal component
    • Avoided grid-wide re-renders
    • Memoized sorting in OrderHistory
  4. Lazy Loading javascript const ProductPage = React.lazy(() => import('./ProductPage')); Improves initial load performance.

docs.title: Backend Optimizations
docs.text:
1. Database Indexing
    • Indexed: Foreign keys, created_at, is_active
    • Reduces query scan time.
  2. Atomic Transactions
    • Ensures: No partial order creation, No inconsistent stock updates
  3. Rate Limiting
    • Applied throttling for: Auth endpoints, Sensitive actions

docs.title: 6. Database Design Overview
docs.text:
• Users
    • Custom user model
    • Email as unique identifier
    • Role flag
  • Products
    • Name, Description, Price, Stock, Category, Image, is_active
  • Cart
    • User, Product, Quantity
  • Orders
    • User, Total, Status, Payment ID, Shipping info
  • OrderItems
    • Order, Product, Quantity, Price snapshot

docs.title: Backend Deployment Flow
docs.text:
1. Push to GitHub
  2. GitHub Actions triggered
  3. SSH into EC2
  4. Pull latest code
  5. Install dependencies
  6. Apply migrations
  7. Restart Gunicorn

docs.title: Nginx Responsibilities
docs.text:
• Reverse proxy
  • HTTPS termination
  • Static file handling

docs.title: Why Gunicorn?
docs.text:
• WSGI server optimized for production
  • Handles concurrency efficiently

docs.title: 8. Security Implementation
docs.text:
• JWT authentication
  • Server-side payment verification
  • Rate limiting
  • Strict CORS
  • Environment-based secrets
  • Secure S3 storage
  • Database not exposed publicly
  • Nginx HTTPS configuration

docs.title: 9. Scalability Considerations
docs.text:
Current scaling strategy:

  • Decoupled frontend/backend
  • S3 media offloading
  • Indexed queries
  • Stateless JWT auth

Future scaling options:

  • Redis caching layer
  • Celery background workers
  • Horizontal EC2 scaling
  • Load balancer
  • CDN caching rules
  • Containerization with Docker

docs.title: 10. CI/CD Pipeline
docs.text:
Automated:

  • Code pull
  • Dependency install
  • Database migration
  • Gunicorn restart

Ensures zero manual deployment errors.

docs.title: 11. Lighthouse & UX Improvements
docs.text:
• Near-perfect Lighthouse score
  • Accessible design
  • Responsive layout
  • Minimal layout shifts
  • Smooth animations
  • PWA installable capability

docs.title: 12. Future Roadmap
docs.text:
• Email notifications (SendGrid / SES)
  • OAuth (Google login)
  • Recommendation engine
  • Advanced analytics dashboard
  • Redis integration
  • Celery task queue
  • Observability (logging + monitoring)

docs.title: 13. Engineering Principles Applied
docs.text:
• Separation of concerns
  • API-first design
  • Atomicity
  • Query optimization
  • Component isolation
  • Clean UI state architecture
  • Cloud-native deployment

docs.title: 14. Summary
docs.text:
Noirel is not a simple CRUD application.

It demonstrates:

  • Full lifecycle engineering
  • Cloud deployment experience
  • Payment integration
  • Performance tuning
  • Security awareness
  • Real-world system architecture

It reflects practical understanding of modern full-stack development at production level.

docs.title: 1. Project Overview
docs.text:
The Personal Portfolio is a high-performance, progressive web application (PWA) engineered to showcase professional work with a premium aesthetic. Built with the React 18 ecosystem, it features a glassmorphism UI, complex animations, and a simulated backend infrastructure for content management.

Unlike static portfolios, this project implements a dynamic content management system using a custom Node.js server, allowing for real-time updates to projects and skills without code deployment.

docs.title: Architectural Pattern
docs.text:
Backend-for-Frontend (BFF) Simulation

The application uses a hybrid architecture: * Client: Single Page Application (SPA) with client-side routing. * Server: A custom Node.js/Express-like layer wrapping JSON Server. * State: Local persistence synchronized with server state.

docs.title: Key Technical Features
docs.text:
• PWA Compliance: Installable on native devices with offline capability.
  • Dynamic Theming: System-preference aware dark/light mode with robust context management.
  • Admin Layer: Secured dashboard for content CRUD operations.

docs.title: Frontend Engineering
docs.text:
• Core: React 18, Vite 5
  • Styling Engine: Tailwind CSS 4 (Atomic CSS)
  • Motion: Framer Motion (Physics-based animations)
  • Navigation: React Router DOM 6.4 (Data routers)
  • Icons: Lucide React (Tree-shakable)

docs.title: Backend & Data
docs.text:
• Runtime: Node.js
  • API Layer: JSON Server (Custom Middleware)
  • File Handling: Multer (Multipart/form-data)
  • Persistence: JSON-based NoSQL simulation

docs.title: 4.1 Glassmorphism & Aesthetics
docs.text: The design system implements a modern "Glass" aesthetic: * Backdrop Filters: Heavy use of backdrop-blur for depth checks. * Responsive Gradients: Dynamic background meshes that react to scroll.

docs.title: 4.2 Interaction Design
docs.text:
• Micro-interactions: Hover states, button presses, and loading skeletons.
  • Page Transitions: AnimatePresence implementation for smooth route changes.

docs.title: Admin Dashboard
docs.text: A protected route (/admin) allows for: * Project Management: upload images, set stack tags, update descriptions. * Analytics: View visit counts (simulated). * Security: Basic auth gate for admin routes.

docs.title: Contact System
docs.text:
• Integration: Formspree API for serverless form handling.
  • Validation: Client-side schema validation before submission.

docs.title: 6. Performance Optimization
docs.text:
• Bundle Splitting: Route-based code splitting via React.lazy.
  • Asset Optimization: WebP format for all portfolio imagery.
  • Atomic CSS: Tailwind ensures CSS bundle size remains constant regardless of app size.
  • PWA Caching: Service workers cache static assets for instant load on repeat visits.

docs.title: Development Environment
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/ashif-ek/portfolio-react.git
cd portfolio-react

1. Start Backend Server

cd server
npm install
# Creates local server at port 5000
npm start

2. Start React Client

cd client
npm install
npm run dev

docs.title: 8. Deployment Strategy
docs.text:
• Frontend: Deployed to Vercel (Edge Network).
    • Correction: Uses Vercel's rewrite rules for SPA routing.
  • Backend: Deployed to Render.com.
    • Health Checks: Implemented to prevents cold-start timeouts.

docs.title: 9. Summary
docs.text: This portfolio represents a Systematic approach to frontend development, prioritizing: 1. Maintainability: Component-driven architecture. 2. Scalability: Externalized data layer. 3. User Experience: Native-like feel via PWA and animations.

docs.title: Salary Reality Checker – AI-Powered Salary Insights
docs.text:
A FAANG-style salary analytics platform that calculates market percentiles, predicts salaries using machine learning, and auto-corrects user input using fuzzy matching.

Built with: * FastAPI * PostgreSQL * SQLAlchemy * scikit-learn ML * React + Vite + TailwindCSS * Recharts + Framer Motion

docs.title: 🔹 Smart Percentile Engine
docs.text: Calculates: * P10 * P25 * P50 (Median) * P75 * P90

docs.title: 🔹 Fuzzy Matching (AI Auto-Correction)
docs.text: Even if user types: py devloper, sofware eng, hyd, blr → App finds the closest valid match.

docs.title: 🔹 ML Salary Prediction
docs.text: If database has no entry: * Trains a Linear Regression model. * Predicts salary based on experience trends.

docs.title: 🔹 Admin Tools
docs.text:
• Upload CSV dataset.
  • Bulk insert into PostgreSQL.

docs.title: 🔹 Modern UI (React)
docs.text:
• Annual / Monthly toggle.
  • Animated charts.
  • Shimmer loading state.
  • Auto-suggest dropdown.
  • Dark & Light mode.

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
• FastAPI
  • SQLAlchemy
  • PostgreSQL
  • scikit-learn
  • RapidFuzz

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
• React (Vite)
  • TailwindCSS
  • Recharts
  • Framer Motion

docs.title: 📡 API Endpoints
docs.text:
• Add Salary POST /salary/add
  • Bulk Upload Dataset POST /salary/bulk
  • Get Salary Insights (Percentiles + ML) GET /salary/insights?job_role=&city=&experience=
  • Predict Salary GET /salary/predict?job_role=&city=&experience=

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
cd backend
pip install -r requirements.txt
uvicorn app.main:app --reload

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev

docs.title: 📊 Demo Preview
docs.text:
• Percentile charts
  • Salary comparison
  • Fuzzy auto-correct input fields
  • ML-powered predictions
  • Elegant FAANG-grade UI

docs.title: Student Management Django Task
docs.text:
A complete student management platform built with Django, featuring authentication, role-based dashboards, course management, email notifications. Includes custom user models (Admin/Student), comprehensive CRUD operations, and a clean MVT architecture.

View on GitHub

docs.title: Time Lens — A New Way to See Time
docs.text:
Time Lens is a mindset tool that transforms how we perceive time. Instead of thinking in hours and minutes, this tool converts real time into a powerful life-model:

  • 1 real day = 1 life-year
  • 1 hour = 1 life-month
  • 1 minute = 1 life-day
  • 1 second = 1 life-hour

The goal is simple: Help you feel the true weight of time so every minute becomes intentional.

Live Demo

docs.title: 🎯 Why This Exists
docs.text:
People say “value your time,” but few explain how to actually value a minute. Time Lens reframes every real minute into a life-day—making even micro-actions meaningful.

Suddenly: * Wasting a minute feels like wasting a day. * Wasting an hour feels like losing a month. * Using a few minutes wisely feels like real progress.

docs.title: What Time Lens Gives You
docs.text:
For any real duration, the tool returns: * Life-days, life-months, and life-years. * A deep insight. * A meaningful action. * A psychological explanation. * A warning if wasted. * A description of who you become if you use it well.

Built with a custom advanced suggestion engine.

docs.title: 🛠️ Tech Stack
docs.text:
• Python
  • FastAPI
  • Uvicorn
  • HTML + CSS
  • JavaScript
  • Deployed on Vercel

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
time-lens/
│
├── main.py
├── requirements.txt
├── render.yaml
│
├── templates/
│   └── index.html
│
└── static/
    └── style.css

docs.title: 📦 Run Locally
docs.text:
1. Create virtual environment bash python -m venv venv

  2. Activate it

    • Windows: powershell venv\Scripts\activate
    • Mac/Linux: bash source venv/bin/activate

  3. Install dependencies bash pip install -r requirements.txt

  4. Start server bash uvicorn main:app --reload

Visit: http://127.0.0.1:8000

View on GitHub

docs.title: Todo Redux Advanced
docs.text:
Todo App is a production-ready Todo application built with React, Vite, and Redux Toolkit. It uses async thunks, debounced search, and throttled actions for efficient state and API management. Features include responsive UI, persistent state, and optimized performance.

docs.title: CLI Commands
docs.text: Reference for command-line tools used in this stack.

docs.title: JavaScript / Node
docs.text:
• npm install: Install dependencies.
  • npm run dev: Start dev server.
  • npm run build: Build for production.
  • npm run lint: Check code quality.

docs.title: Python / Django
docs.text:
• python manage.py runserver: Start dev server.
  • python manage.py makemigrations: Create DB migrations.
  • python manage.py migrate: Apply DB migrations.
  • python manage.py createsuperuser: Create admin account.

docs.title: Python / FastAPI
docs.text: • uvicorn main:app --reload: Start server with hot reload.

docs.title: Configuration Options
docs.text:
Detailed reference for environment variables.

Variable Description Required Default DEBUG Enable debug mode No False SECRET_KEY App secret for signing Yes - DATABASE_URL DB Connection string Yes - ALLOWED_HOSTS Comma-separated domains Yes * CORS_ORIGIN Frontend URL for CORS Yes - PORT Server port No 8000

See Configuration for setup instructions.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Terms used across the project documentation.

  • JWT (JSON Web Token): Compact URL-safe means of representing claims to be transferred between two parties.
  • MVT (Model-View-Template): Architectural pattern used by Django.
  • SPA (Single Page Application): Web app that interacts with the web browser by dynamically rewriting the current web page with new data from the web server.
  • ORM (Object-Relational Mapping): Technique for converting data between incompatible type systems using object-oriented programming languages.
  • Hook: Special function in React that lets you “hook into” React features.
